symptoms.symptom_name: Thick white curd-like (pseudomembranous) plaques over buccal mucosa, palate and posterior pharynx

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confluent removable curd-like plaques across multiple mucosal sites are the hallmark of pseudomembranous candidiasis, especially after antibiotics.
Widespread buccal and palatal involvement rather than isolated tonsillar exudate argues against bacterial pharyngitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning odynophagia in the mouth and throat, rapidly worsening over 24 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense burning odynophagia with oesophageal spread is typical, but pain severity alone is non-specific and does not exclude other oesophageal causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent completed 14-day course of broad-spectrum antibiotics (cephalexin) for a urinary infection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Broad-spectrum antibiotics suppress commensal flora and are a leading precipitant of Candida overgrowth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete inability to swallow food, fluids or saliva for ~12 hours with cessation of oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Total dysphagia with 12 hours of no intake is the direct driver of dehydration and the key determinant of urgent disposition.
Sudden total dysphagia including saliva can signal an obstructing or alternative oesophageal cause that must be examined in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plaques scrape off with a tongue depressor to reveal a raw, easily-bleeding, intensely erythematous base

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A removable plaque leaving a bleeding erythematous base is characteristic of pseudomembranous candidiasis and distinguishes it from leukoplakia — a clinician-performed manoeuvre, sealed from telehealth.
Leukoplakia is chronic, painless and adherent (does not scrape off); the acute, painful, removable plaque excludes it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Impaired swallow with marked pain response on clinician assessment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A directly observed painful, impaired swallow supports significant oropharyngeal/oesophageal disease — a clinician assessment not obtainable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Benign abdominal examination (no obstruction or acute abdominal signs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A benign abdomen makes a distal obstructive/acute abdominal process less likely, but does not exclude a proximal oesophageal cause — a clinician examination, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dehydration on clinician assessment — dry mucous membranes with tachycardia and borderline-low blood pressure (HR 102, BP 105/65, weight 58 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dry mucous membranes with early tachycardia and borderline-low blood pressure are objective dehydration — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers dehydration from the nurse-reported dry mouth, weakness and 12-hour intake cessation, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade temperature (37.4 C) without high fever or rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of high fever argues against a bacterial pharyngitis; a clinician-measured value, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No stridor, drooling with muffled voice, respiratory distress or neck swelling (airway not compromised)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: supraglottic/deep-space airway emergency (epiglottitis, quinsy, Ludwig angina)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quiet breathing, a normal voice and no drooling or neck swelling make an airway emergency unlikely — this is why the case is urgent in-person rather than a call-000 airway emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: No grouped vesicles or punched-out ulcers on the mucosa

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: herpes simplex / viral oesophagitis or stomatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Removable curd-like plaques rather than grouped vesicles or punched-out ulcers argue against a primary herpetic process — best confirmed on in-person examination.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Post-antibiotic onset, confluent removable curd-like plaques and a bleeding base on scraping together make antibiotic-associated candidiasis the leading mucosal diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute dehydration from cessation of oral intake in a frail older adult
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Total dysphagia with 12 hours of no intake plus dry mucous membranes and early haemodynamic change is the time-critical complication that determines urgent in-person disposition, independent of the mucosal cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person oropharyngeal examination (with plaque scraping) at urgent medical review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms pseudomembranous candidiasis, distinguishes it from leukoplakia and herpetic disease, and helps exclude an obstructing/alternative cause; cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine (renal function) at in-person assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies dehydration and detects acute kidney injury in a frail older adult who has had no oral intake for ~12 hours; guides intravenous fluid replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Consideration of oesophagoscopy / ENT or gastroenterology review if dysphagia persists or an obstructing/oesophageal cause is suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate if the dysphagia does not resolve with antifungal therapy and rehydration or if an obstruction/severe oesophagitis is suspected — a downstream in-person decision.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical antibiotics for presumed bacterial pharyngitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: There is no evidence of bacterial pharyngitis; recent broad-spectrum antibiotics precipitated the candidiasis and further antibiotics would worsen it.
